declarations.doc: Public project name used by documentation and the website.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.projectName

declarations.doc: A small status vocabulary for roadmap items and generated documentation.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Status

declarations.doc: A weighted already-known inequality, represented by a nonnegative expression.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.WeightedIneq

declarations.doc: Evaluate a list of weighted inequalities as a real linear combination.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.evalCombination

declarations.doc: The formal entropy expression represented by a rational linear combination of weighted inequalities.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.combinationExpr

declarations.doc: Evaluating the formal combination expression agrees with the older semantic combination function.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.eval_combinationExpr

declarations.doc: An exact rational-expression check for a certificate decomposition. Since `EntropyExpr` is a `Finsupp`, this equality compares normalized sparse rational coefficients atom by atom.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.DecompositionMatches

declarations.doc: A certificate for a target expression.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.Cert

declarations.doc: A nonnegative linear combination of nonnegative interpreted expressions is nonnegative. This is the algebraic engine behind certificate soundness.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.evalCombination_nonneg

declarations.doc: Certificate soundness: if the target evaluates to the certified combination and each ingredient is nonnegative under the semantic interpretation, then the target inequality is nonnegative.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.sound

declarations.doc: Certificate soundness from exact rational expression equality. This is the preferred route for checked certificates: first compare formal entropy expressions, then evaluate.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.sound_of_decompositionMatches

declarations.doc: An untrusted certificate step as it might be parsed from an external source. No soundness proof should use a `RawStep` directly.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.RawStep

declarations.doc: An untrusted certificate as it might be parsed from an external source. It must be validated before it can justify an entropy inequality.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.RawCert

declarations.doc: Forget a raw step into the older generic weighted-inequality format.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.RawStep.toWeightedIneq

declarations.doc: Forget a raw certificate into the older generic certificate format.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.RawCert.toCert

declarations.doc: A checked certificate step. Its coefficient has been proved nonnegative, and its ingredient is one of the primitive Shannon inequalities.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.CheckedStep

declarations.doc: The entropy expression used by a checked step.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.CheckedStep.expr

declarations.doc: Forget a checked step into the older generic weighted-inequality format.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.CheckedStep.toWeightedIneq

declarations.doc: The coefficient of a checked step is nonnegative as a rational number.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.CheckedStep.coeff_rat_nonneg

declarations.doc: The coefficient of a checked step is nonnegative after casting to `Real`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.CheckedStep.coeff_real_nonneg

declarations.doc: The ingredient expression of a checked step is nonnegative under any Shannon valuation.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.CheckedStep.expr_nonneg

declarations.doc: Validate a raw step against a proposed primitive Shannon inequality. The check accepts exactly when the raw coefficient is nonnegative and the raw expression is equal to the primitive expression as a normalized sparse entropy expression. The resulting `CheckedStep` carries the nonnegative coefficient as an `NNRat`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.RawStep.toCheckedStep?

declarations.doc: Validate raw steps against a parallel list of primitive Shannon inequalities. This is intentionally list-shaped for the first certificate layer: external certificate import can parse candidate primitive tags separately, and this function checks that every raw expression and coefficient agrees with those tags.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.checkStepsAgainstPrimitives?

declarations.doc: A checked certificate for a target entropy inequality.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.CheckedCert

declarations.doc: Forget a checked certificate into the older generic certificate format.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.CheckedCert.toCert

declarations.doc: Every checked step in a checked certificate is semantically nonnegative.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.CheckedCert.steps_nonneg

declarations.doc: Checked certificate soundness under any abstract Shannon entropy valuation.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.CheckedCert.sound

declarations.doc: Validate a raw certificate against a list of proposed primitive Shannon ingredients. The validator checks each raw step against the corresponding primitive tag, then checks exact rational equality between the target and the resulting checked decomposition.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.RawCert.toCheckedCert?

declarations.doc: Any successfully validated raw certificate proves its raw target expression.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.RawCert.sound_of_toCheckedCert?_eq_some

declarations.doc: Any raw certificate whose validation returns some checked certificate proves its raw target expression. This is the ergonomic form used by certificate demos: once a concrete raw certificate has a validation theorem of the form `(cert.toCheckedCert? primitives).isSome`, callers do not need to split the resulting option by hand.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.RawCert.sound_of_toCheckedCert?_isSome

declarations.doc: The entropy-extension expression `H(S ∪ {i}) - H(S)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.Monotonicity.expr

declarations.doc: The entropy-extension expression is the conditional-entropy primitive.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.Monotonicity.expr_eq_condEntropy

declarations.doc: The primitive Shannon inequality used by the monotonicity certificate.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.Monotonicity.primitive

declarations.doc: The checked step used by the monotonicity certificate.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.Monotonicity.checkedStep

declarations.doc: The checked step has exactly the target monotonicity expression.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.Monotonicity.checkedStep_decomposition_matches

declarations.doc: A proof-carrying checked certificate for one-variable entropy monotonicity.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.Monotonicity.checkedCert

declarations.doc: Soundness of the checked monotonicity certificate.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.Monotonicity.checkedCert_sound

declarations.doc: The raw step used by the monotonicity certificate.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.Monotonicity.rawStep

declarations.doc: The raw monotonicity step validates to the checked monotonicity step.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.Monotonicity.rawStep_toCheckedStep?_eq_some

declarations.doc: The raw monotonicity step list validates against the primitive tag list.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.Monotonicity.checkStepsAgainstPrimitives_eq_some

declarations.doc: A raw external-style certificate for one-variable entropy monotonicity.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.Monotonicity.rawCert

declarations.doc: The raw monotonicity certificate validates against its primitive tag.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.Monotonicity.rawCert_toCheckedCert?_isSome

declarations.doc: Running the raw monotonicity validator gives a sound certificate.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.Monotonicity.sound_from_validator

declarations.doc: One-variable entropy monotonicity for every abstract Shannon entropy valuation.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.Monotonicity.entropy_insert_monotonicity

declarations.doc: The entropy subadditivity expression `H(A) + H(B) - H(A ∪ B)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.Subadditivity.expr

declarations.doc: Subadditivity is `I(A;B | ∅) + H(∅)` as an entropy expression.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.Subadditivity.expr_eq_cmi_empty_add_emptyEntropy

declarations.doc: The conditional mutual-information primitive used by the certificate.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.Subadditivity.cmiPrimitive

declarations.doc: The empty-entropy primitive used to cancel the extra `-H(∅)` CMI term.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.Subadditivity.emptyPrimitive

declarations.doc: Checked CMI certificate step with coefficient `1`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.Subadditivity.checkedCmiStep

declarations.doc: Checked empty-entropy certificate step with coefficient `1`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.Subadditivity.checkedEmptyStep

declarations.doc: The checked step list has exactly the target subadditivity expression.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.Subadditivity.checkedSteps_decomposition_matches

declarations.doc: Checked certificate for entropy subadditivity.

declarations.doc: Soundness of the checked subadditivity certificate.

declarations.doc: Raw CMI certificate step for subadditivity.

declarations.doc: Raw empty-entropy certificate step for subadditivity.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.Subadditivity.rawEmptyStep

declarations.doc: Raw certificate for entropy subadditivity.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.Subadditivity.rawCert

declarations.doc: The raw CMI step validates to the intended checked step.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.Subadditivity.rawCmiStep_toCheckedStep?_eq_some

declarations.doc: The raw empty-entropy step validates to the intended checked step.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.Subadditivity.rawEmptyStep_toCheckedStep?_eq_some

declarations.doc: The raw subadditivity step list validates to the intended checked step list.

declarations.doc: The raw subadditivity certificate is accepted by the validator.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.Subadditivity.rawCert_toCheckedCert?_isSome

declarations.doc: Soundness obtained through the raw certificate validator.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.Subadditivity.sound_from_validator

declarations.doc: Entropy subadditivity, proved by the checked certificate.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Certificate.Subadditivity.entropy_subadditivity

declarations.doc: The formal entropy-expression form of submodularity.

declarations.doc: An entropy atom is a finite set of random-variable names.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.EntropyAtom

declarations.doc: The empty entropy atom, conventionally written `H(∅)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.EntropyAtom.empty

declarations.doc: A formal rational linear combination of entropy atoms.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.EntropyExpr

declarations.doc: The expression with coefficient `1` on one entropy atom.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.EntropyExpr.atom

declarations.doc: The formal entropy expression `H(∅)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.EntropyExpr.empty

declarations.doc: Cast a rational entropy expression to real coefficients.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.EntropyExpr.realCoeffs

declarations.doc: Evaluate a formal entropy expression against any assignment of real values to entropy atoms.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.EntropyExpr.eval

declarations.doc: An interpretation of entropy atoms respects the empty-entropy convention if it assigns value `0` to `H(∅)`. This is deliberately a separate predicate rather than a property of every function `EntropyAtom Var -> Real`: the next certificate layer can require this assumption, and the later concrete finite-family semantics should prove it.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.EntropyExpr.RespectsEmpty

declarations.doc: Casting coefficients to `Real` sends the zero expression to zero.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.EntropyExpr.realCoeffs_zero

declarations.doc: Casting coefficients to `Real` sends a single rational coefficient to a single real one.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.EntropyExpr.realCoeffs_single

declarations.doc: Casting coefficients to `Real` commutes with addition.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.EntropyExpr.realCoeffs_add

declarations.doc: Casting coefficients to `Real` commutes with negation.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.EntropyExpr.realCoeffs_neg

declarations.doc: Casting coefficients to `Real` commutes with subtraction.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.EntropyExpr.realCoeffs_sub

declarations.doc: Casting coefficients to `Real` commutes with rational scalar multiplication.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.EntropyExpr.realCoeffs_smul

declarations.doc: The zero formal expression evaluates to zero under every interpretation.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.EntropyExpr.eval_zero

declarations.doc: Evaluation is additive in the formal entropy expression.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.EntropyExpr.eval_add

declarations.doc: Evaluation commutes with subtracting formal entropy expressions.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.EntropyExpr.eval_sub

declarations.doc: Evaluation commutes with negating a formal entropy expression.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.EntropyExpr.eval_neg

declarations.doc: Evaluation commutes with rational scalar multiplication.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.EntropyExpr.eval_smul

declarations.doc: A single entropy atom evaluates to the value assigned to that atom.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.EntropyExpr.eval_atom

declarations.doc: The empty entropy expression evaluates to the value assigned to the empty atom.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.EntropyExpr.eval_empty

declarations.doc: Under the empty-entropy convention, the expression `H(∅)` evaluates to zero.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.EntropyExpr.eval_empty_eq_zero_of_respectsEmpty

declarations.doc: For an arbitrary atom interpretation, respecting the empty-entropy convention is equivalent to evaluating the formal expression `H(∅)` as zero.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.EntropyExpr.respectsEmpty_iff_eval_empty_eq_zero

declarations.doc: An abstract Shannon entropy valuation assigns a real value to every entropy atom and records the basic Shannon-type facts needed by the certificate layer. The fields are intentionally semantic assumptions rather than proved facts: concrete finite-probability models should instantiate this structure later.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.ShannonEntropyVal

declarations.doc: Evaluate an entropy expression using an abstract Shannon entropy valuation.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.ShannonEntropyVal.eval

declarations.doc: A Shannon entropy valuation respects the empty-entropy convention.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.ShannonEntropyVal.respectsEmpty

declarations.doc: The empty atom has value zero under a Shannon entropy valuation.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.ShannonEntropyVal.apply_empty

declarations.doc: The zero expression evaluates to zero under a Shannon entropy valuation.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.ShannonEntropyVal.eval_zero

declarations.doc: A single entropy atom evaluates to the value assigned by the valuation.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.ShannonEntropyVal.eval_atom

declarations.doc: The empty entropy expression evaluates to zero under a Shannon entropy valuation.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.ShannonEntropyVal.eval_empty

declarations.doc: The primitive empty-entropy expression `H(empty)`.

declarations.doc: The primitive conditional-entropy expression `H(S ∪ {i}) - H(S)`. When evaluated by a Shannon entropy valuation and `i ∉ S`, this should be nonnegative.

declarations.doc: The primitive conditional-mutual-information expression `H(A,C) + H(B,C) - H(A,B,C) - H(C)`. When evaluated by a Shannon entropy valuation, this should be nonnegative.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.PrimitiveIneq.condMutualInfo

declarations.doc: Evaluation formula for the primitive empty-entropy expression.

declarations.doc: Evaluation formula for primitive conditional entropy.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.PrimitiveIneq.eval_condEntropy

declarations.doc: Evaluation formula for primitive conditional mutual information.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.PrimitiveIneq.eval_condMutualInfo

declarations.doc: Shannon-valuation evaluation formula for the empty-entropy primitive.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.PrimitiveIneq.shannonEval_emptyEntropy

declarations.doc: Shannon-valuation evaluation formula for primitive conditional entropy.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.PrimitiveIneq.shannonEval_condEntropy

declarations.doc: Shannon-valuation evaluation formula for primitive conditional mutual information.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.PrimitiveIneq.shannonEval_condMutualInfo

declarations.doc: The empty-entropy primitive evaluates to a nonnegative quantity.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.PrimitiveIneq.emptyEntropy_nonneg

declarations.doc: Primitive conditional entropy is nonnegative under a Shannon entropy valuation when the adjoined variable is not already present.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.PrimitiveIneq.condEntropy_nonneg

declarations.doc: Primitive conditional mutual information is nonnegative under a Shannon entropy valuation.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.PrimitiveIneq.condMutualInfo_nonneg

declarations.doc: The primitive Shannon inequalities that the first checked certificate layer is allowed to use as trusted ingredients.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.PrimitiveIneq.Kind

declarations.doc: The entropy expression represented by a primitive Shannon inequality.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.PrimitiveIneq.Kind.expr

declarations.doc: Every primitive Shannon inequality is sound under a Shannon entropy valuation.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.PrimitiveIneq.Kind.nonneg

declarations.doc: Each atom of a `PMF` has nonnegative real mass.
declarations.name: PMF.toReal_nonneg

declarations.doc: Each atom of a `PMF` has real mass at most one.
declarations.name: PMF.toReal_le_one

declarations.doc: On a finite type, the real masses of a `PMF` sum to one.
declarations.name: PMF.sum_toReal

declarations.doc: If `f` is injective, pushing a PMF forward along `f` preserves the mass of each source atom at its image.
declarations.name: PMF.map_apply_of_injective

declarations.doc: If a target atom is outside the range of a map, then the pushed-forward PMF assigns zero mass to that atom.
declarations.name: PMF.map_apply_eq_zero_of_notMem_range

declarations.doc: Pushing a PMF forward along an equivalence preserves the mass of each atom at the renamed atom.
declarations.name: PMF.map_apply_equiv

declarations.doc: Shannon entropy, in nats, of a finite probability mass function.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropy

declarations.doc: The defining finite-sum formula for Shannon entropy.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropy_eq_sum

declarations.doc: Shannon entropy is nonnegative for finite PMFs.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropy_nonneg

declarations.doc: Entropy is invariant under relabeling the alphabet by an equivalence. This is one of the basic sanity checks for finite entropy: only the masses matter, not the names of the atoms.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropy_map_equiv

declarations.doc: Entropy is invariant under injective relabeling into a larger finite alphabet. Atoms outside the image of the relabeling have zero pushed-forward mass.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropy_map_injective

declarations.doc: A deterministic finite PMF has entropy zero.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropy_pure

declarations.doc: Entropy of a finite-valued random variable under a discrete law.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropyOf

declarations.doc: Entropy of the identity random variable is the entropy of the original law.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropyOf_id

declarations.doc: Relabeling a finite-valued random variable by an equivalence preserves its entropy.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropyOf_comp_equiv

declarations.doc: Applying an injective relabeling to a finite-valued random variable preserves its entropy.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropyOf_comp_injective

declarations.doc: Joint entropy is entropy of a joint finite distribution.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.jointEntropy

declarations.doc: Swapping the two coordinates of a joint law preserves entropy.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropy_map_swap

declarations.doc: Reassociating a left-associated triple alphabet preserves entropy. This records that `H((A, B), C)` and `H(A, B, C)` are the same entropy after the canonical product reassociation equivalence.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropy_map_prodAssoc

declarations.doc: Reassociating a right-associated triple alphabet back to the left preserves entropy.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropy_map_prodAssoc_symm

declarations.doc: Swapping the two coordinates of a joint law preserves joint entropy.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.jointEntropy_map_swap

declarations.doc: Joint entropy of two finite-valued random variables under a discrete law.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.jointEntropyOf

declarations.doc: Joint entropy of two random variables is invariant under swapping their order.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.jointEntropyOf_swap

declarations.doc: Entropy of three finite-valued random variables is invariant under reassociating the product alphabet.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropyOf_prodAssoc

declarations.doc: Finite Shannon entropy is bounded above by the logarithm of the alphabet size. The proof is Jensen's inequality for the concave function `Real.negMulLog`, applied with uniform weights over the finite alphabet.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropy_le_log_card

declarations.doc: The uniform distribution reaches the finite entropy upper bound. Mathematically, this is the equality case `H(U_alpha) = log |alpha|` for the uniform law on a nonempty finite alphabet.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropy_uniformOfFintype

declarations.doc: The first marginal of a finite joint PMF.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.fstMarginal

declarations.doc: The second marginal of a finite joint PMF.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.sndMarginal

declarations.doc: Taking the first marginal after pushing a PMF forward to a pair is the same as pushing forward by the first coordinate of that pair-valued map.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.fstMarginal_map

declarations.doc: Taking the second marginal after pushing a PMF forward to a pair is the same as pushing forward by the second coordinate of that pair-valued map.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.sndMarginal_map

declarations.doc: For a joint law built from random variables `(X, Y)`, the first marginal is the law of `X`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.fstMarginal_map_pair

declarations.doc: For a joint law built from random variables `(X, Y)`, the second marginal is the law of `Y`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.sndMarginal_map_pair

declarations.doc: Swapping the two coordinates turns the first marginal into the original second marginal.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.fstMarginal_map_swap

declarations.doc: Swapping the two coordinates turns the second marginal into the original first marginal.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.sndMarginal_map_swap

declarations.doc: The mass of the first marginal is the sum of the joint masses over the second coordinate.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.fstMarginal_apply

declarations.doc: The mass of the second marginal is the sum of the joint masses over the first coordinate.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.sndMarginal_apply

declarations.doc: The marginal on the first and second coordinates of a finite triple PMF.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.fstSndMarginal

declarations.doc: Projection formula for the first-second marginal of a pushed-forward triple law.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.fstSndMarginal_map

declarations.doc: For a joint law built from `(X, Y, Z)`, the first-second marginal is the joint law of `(X, Y)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.fstSndMarginal_map_triple

declarations.doc: The marginal on the first and third coordinates of a finite triple PMF.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.fstThirdMarginal

declarations.doc: The marginal on the second and third coordinates of a finite triple PMF.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.sndThirdMarginal

declarations.doc: The marginal on the third coordinate of a finite triple PMF.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.thirdMarginal

declarations.doc: Projection formula for the first-third marginal of a pushed-forward triple law. This is the triple analogue of `fstMarginal_map`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.fstThirdMarginal_map

declarations.doc: Projection formula for the second-third marginal of a pushed-forward triple law. The product `alpha × beta × gamma` is right-associated in Lean.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.sndThirdMarginal_map

declarations.doc: Projection formula for the third-coordinate marginal of a pushed-forward triple law.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.thirdMarginal_map

declarations.doc: For a joint law built from `(X, Y, Z)`, the first-third marginal is the joint law of `(X, Z)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.fstThirdMarginal_map_triple

declarations.doc: For a joint law built from `(X, Y, Z)`, the second-third marginal is the joint law of `(Y, Z)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.sndThirdMarginal_map_triple

declarations.doc: For a joint law built from `(X, Y, Z)`, the third marginal is the law of `Z`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.thirdMarginal_map_triple

declarations.doc: After swapping the first two coordinates of `(A, B, C)`, the first-third marginal is the original second-third marginal.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.fstThirdMarginal_map_swap12

declarations.doc: After swapping the first two coordinates of `(A, B, C)`, the second-third marginal is the original first-third marginal.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.sndThirdMarginal_map_swap12

declarations.doc: Swapping the first two coordinates leaves the third marginal unchanged.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.thirdMarginal_map_swap12

declarations.doc: The mass of the first-third marginal is the sum of the triple joint masses over the second coordinate.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.fstThirdMarginal_apply

declarations.doc: The mass of the second-third marginal is the sum of the triple joint masses over the first coordinate.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.sndThirdMarginal_apply

declarations.doc: The mass of the third marginal is the sum of the triple joint masses over the first two coordinates.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.thirdMarginal_apply

declarations.doc: A joint atom mass is bounded by the mass of its first marginal atom. This is the basic support/domination fact behind conditioning on the first coordinate.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.apply_le_fstMarginal

declarations.doc: A joint atom mass is bounded by the mass of its second marginal atom.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.apply_le_sndMarginal

declarations.doc: In a triple law, a full atom is bounded by the corresponding `(first, third)` marginal atom.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.apply_le_fstThirdMarginal

declarations.doc: In a triple law, a full atom is bounded by the corresponding `(second, third)` marginal atom.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.apply_le_sndThirdMarginal

declarations.doc: In a triple law, a full atom is bounded by the mass of its third-coordinate marginal atom. This is the support fact needed for conditioning on `gamma`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.apply_le_thirdMarginal

declarations.doc: If a first marginal atom has zero mass, then every joint atom over that first coordinate has zero mass.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.apply_eq_zero_of_fstMarginal_eq_zero

declarations.doc: If a second marginal atom has zero mass, then every joint atom over that second coordinate has zero mass.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.apply_eq_zero_of_sndMarginal_eq_zero

declarations.doc: Nonzero joint mass forces the corresponding first marginal mass to be nonzero.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.fstMarginal_ne_zero_of_apply_ne_zero

declarations.doc: Nonzero joint mass forces the corresponding second marginal mass to be nonzero.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.sndMarginal_ne_zero_of_apply_ne_zero

declarations.doc: If a `(first, third)` marginal atom has zero mass, then every triple atom over that pair has zero mass.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.apply_eq_zero_of_fstThirdMarginal_eq_zero

declarations.doc: If a `(second, third)` marginal atom has zero mass, then every triple atom over that pair has zero mass.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.apply_eq_zero_of_sndThirdMarginal_eq_zero

declarations.doc: If a third marginal atom has zero mass, then every triple atom over that third coordinate has zero mass.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.apply_eq_zero_of_thirdMarginal_eq_zero

declarations.doc: Nonzero triple mass forces the corresponding `(first, third)` marginal mass to be nonzero.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.fstThirdMarginal_ne_zero_of_apply_ne_zero

declarations.doc: Nonzero triple mass forces the corresponding `(second, third)` marginal mass to be nonzero.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.sndThirdMarginal_ne_zero_of_apply_ne_zero

declarations.doc: Nonzero triple mass forces the corresponding third-coordinate marginal mass to be nonzero.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.thirdMarginal_ne_zero_of_apply_ne_zero

declarations.doc: Conditional entropy `H(alpha | beta)` of a finite joint PMF on `alpha × beta`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropy

declarations.doc: Mutual information `I(alpha; beta)` of a finite joint PMF on `alpha × beta`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfo

declarations.doc: Conditional mutual information `I(alpha; beta | gamma)` of a finite joint PMF on `alpha × beta × gamma`, where the product is Lean's right-associated product.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condMutualInfo

declarations.doc: Conditional entropy `H(X | Y)` for finite-valued random variables.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropyOf

declarations.doc: Mutual information `I(X; Y)` for finite-valued random variables.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfoOf

declarations.doc: Conditional mutual information `I(X; Y | Z)` for finite-valued random variables.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condMutualInfoOf

declarations.doc: Mutual information is symmetric under swapping the two coordinates of a joint law.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfo_map_swap

declarations.doc: Mutual information of finite-valued random variables is symmetric: `I(Y;X) = I(X;Y)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfoOf_swap

declarations.doc: Conditional mutual information is symmetric in its first two coordinates: `I(B;A | C) = I(A;B | C)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condMutualInfo_map_swap12

declarations.doc: Conditional mutual information of finite-valued random variables is symmetric in the first two variables: `I(Y;X | Z) = I(X;Y | Z)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condMutualInfoOf_swap

declarations.doc: Entropy of the first coordinate projection is entropy of the first marginal.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropyOf_fst

declarations.doc: Entropy of the second coordinate projection is entropy of the second marginal.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropyOf_snd

declarations.doc: The joint entropy of the two coordinate projections of an existing pair law is the entropy of that pair law itself.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.jointEntropyOf_fst_snd

declarations.doc: Conditional entropy of the coordinate projections of a pair law recovers the PMF-level conditional entropy of that pair law.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropyOf_fst_snd

declarations.doc: Mutual information of the coordinate projections of a pair law recovers the PMF-level mutual information of that pair law.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfoOf_fst_snd

declarations.doc: Entropy of the third coordinate projection is entropy of the third marginal.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropyOf_third

declarations.doc: Joint entropy of the first and third coordinate projections is the entropy of the first-third marginal.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.jointEntropyOf_fst_third

declarations.doc: Joint entropy of the second and third coordinate projections is the entropy of the second-third marginal.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.jointEntropyOf_snd_third

declarations.doc: Conditional mutual information of the coordinate projections of a triple law recovers the PMF-level `I(first; second | third)` quantity.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condMutualInfoOf_fst_snd_third

declarations.doc: Unfolding lemma for the entropy-identity definition of conditional entropy.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropy_eq

declarations.doc: Unfolding lemma for the entropy-identity definition of mutual information.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfo_eq

declarations.doc: Unfolding lemma for the entropy-identity definition of conditional mutual information.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condMutualInfo_eq

declarations.doc: Conditional entropy chain rule for a joint PMF: `H(A,B) = H(B) + H(A | B)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropy_eq_entropy_sndMarginal_add_condEntropy

declarations.doc: Swapped conditional entropy chain rule for a joint PMF: `H(A,B) = H(A) + H(B | A)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropy_eq_entropy_fstMarginal_add_condEntropy_swap

declarations.doc: Conditional entropy chain rule for finite-valued random variables: `H(X,Y) = H(Y) + H(X | Y)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.jointEntropyOf_eq_entropyOf_add_condEntropyOf

declarations.doc: Swapped conditional entropy chain rule for finite-valued random variables: `H(X,Y) = H(X) + H(Y | X)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.jointEntropyOf_eq_entropyOf_add_condEntropyOf_swap

declarations.doc: Random-variable conditional entropy rewrites to joint entropy minus entropy of the conditioning variable.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropyOf_eq

declarations.doc: Random-variable mutual information rewrites to the standard entropy identity `H(X) + H(Y) - H(X,Y)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfoOf_eq

declarations.doc: Random-variable conditional mutual information rewrites to the entropy identity `H(X,Z) + H(Y,Z) - H(Z) - H(X,Y,Z)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condMutualInfoOf_eq

declarations.doc: Self-information of an atom under a finite PMF, in nats. It is `-log p(a)` when the real mass of `a` is nonzero, and `0` on zero-mass atoms. The zero branch is the usual entropy convention that zero-probability outcomes contribute no expected information.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.selfInfo

declarations.doc: A zero-mass atom has zero self-information by convention.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.selfInfo_of_toReal_eq_zero

declarations.doc: A nonzero-mass atom has self-information `-log p(a)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.selfInfo_of_toReal_ne_zero

declarations.doc: Multiplying self-information by the atom mass recovers the `Real.negMulLog` entropy summand.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.toReal_mul_selfInfo

declarations.doc: Semantic bridge for finite entropy: the finite-sum definition of `Shannon.entropy` is the expected self-information with respect to `PMF.toMeasure`. Informally, this is the textbook identity `H(P) = E[-log P(X)]`, with the zero-mass convention handled by `selfInfo`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropy_eq_integral_selfInfo

declarations.doc: Conditional law of the first coordinate given a nonzero second-coordinate atom. Mathematically, this is the finite PMF `P_{A | B=b}(a) = P_{A,B}(a,b) / P_B(b)`. The proof argument `hb : sndMarginal p b ≠ 0` is part of the API so that zero-probability conditioning atoms do not receive a fake default distribution.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condFstGivenSnd

declarations.doc: Atom formula for the conditional law: `P_{A | B=b}(a) = P_{A,B}(a,b) / P_B(b)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condFstGivenSnd_apply

declarations.doc: The conditional PMF does not depend on which proof of nonzero marginal mass was used to construct it.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condFstGivenSnd_proof_irrel

declarations.doc: An atom has zero conditional mass exactly when the corresponding joint atom has zero mass.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condFstGivenSnd_apply_eq_zero_iff

declarations.doc: An atom has nonzero conditional mass exactly when the corresponding joint atom has nonzero mass.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condFstGivenSnd_apply_ne_zero_iff

declarations.doc: The support of `P_{A | B=b}` is the fiber of the joint support over `b`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.support_condFstGivenSnd

declarations.doc: Joint mass factors as conditional mass times the conditioning marginal: `P_{A | B=b}(a) * P_B(b) = P_{A,B}(a,b)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condFstGivenSnd_mul_sndMarginal

declarations.doc: Joint mass factors as the conditioning marginal times conditional mass: `P_B(b) * P_{A | B=b}(a) = P_{A,B}(a,b)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.sndMarginal_mul_condFstGivenSnd

declarations.doc: Real-valued version of the joint factorization: `P_B(b) * P_{A | B=b}(a) = P_{A,B}(a,b)` after converting masses to `Real`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.sndMarginal_toReal_mul_condFstGivenSnd_toReal

declarations.doc: Entropy of the conditional fiber, with explicit zero-mass branch. This helper is intended for expected conditional entropy formulas. If `P_B(b)=0`, the branch is the number `0`, not the entropy of an arbitrary default conditional PMF.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropyFstGivenSnd

declarations.doc: Zero-marginal conditioning atoms contribute zero to conditional-fiber entropy.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropyFstGivenSnd_of_sndMarginal_eq_zero

declarations.doc: On nonzero conditioning atoms, `condEntropyFstGivenSnd` is the entropy of the canonical conditional PMF `P_{A | B=b}`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropyFstGivenSnd_of_sndMarginal_ne_zero

declarations.doc: Per-fiber conditional entropy identity. For each conditioning atom `b`, `p_B(b) * H(P_{A | B=b}) = sum_a h(p(a,b)) - h(p_B(b))`, where `h(x) = -x log x`. If `p_B(b)=0`, the left side uses the explicit zero branch of `condEntropyFstGivenSnd`, and the right side is zero because every joint atom over `b` has zero mass.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.sndMarginal_toReal_mul_condEntropyFstGivenSnd

declarations.doc: Expected conditional entropy formula for the finite conditional-law API: `H(A | B) = sum_b p_B(b) * H(P_{A | B=b})`. The right-hand side is written using `condEntropyFstGivenSnd`, whose value is `0` on zero-marginal conditioning atoms and the entropy of the canonical conditional PMF on nonzero atoms.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropy_eq_sum_sndMarginal_mul_condEntropyFstGivenSnd

declarations.doc: The right-associated law `P_{A,B,C}` viewed as a law on `((A, B), C)`. This is the law whose conditional fibers over `C=c` are joint laws on `A × B`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.pairThirdLaw

declarations.doc: Atom formula for the reassociated law `((A,B),C)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.pairThirdLaw_apply

declarations.doc: The second marginal of the reassociated law `((A,B),C)` is the law of `C`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.sndMarginal_pairThirdLaw

declarations.doc: Reassociating `(A,B,C)` as `((A,B),C)` preserves entropy.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropy_pairThirdLaw

declarations.doc: Conditional joint law of the first two coordinates given a nonzero third coordinate atom. Mathematically, this is `P_{A,B | C=c}(a,b) = P_{A,B,C}(a,b,c) / P_C(c)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condFstSndGivenThird

declarations.doc: Atom formula for `P_{A,B | C=c}`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condFstSndGivenThird_apply

declarations.doc: Joint mass factors through the conditional joint law: `P_C(c) * P_{A,B|C=c}(a,b) = P_{A,B,C}(a,b,c)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.thirdMarginal_mul_condFstSndGivenThird

declarations.doc: Real-valued joint factorization for the conditional joint law: `P_C(c) * P_{A,B|C=c}(a,b) = P_{A,B,C}(a,b,c)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.thirdMarginal_toReal_mul_condFstSndGivenThird_toReal

declarations.doc: The second marginal of the `(A,C)` law is the law of `C`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.sndMarginal_fstThirdMarginal

declarations.doc: The second marginal of the `(B,C)` law is the law of `C`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.sndMarginal_sndThirdMarginal

declarations.doc: The first marginal of `P_{A,B|C=c}` is the conditional law `P_{A|C=c}`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.fstMarginal_condFstSndGivenThird

declarations.doc: The second marginal of `P_{A,B|C=c}` is the conditional law `P_{B|C=c}`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.sndMarginal_condFstSndGivenThird

declarations.doc: The conditional law of `(A,B)` given `C=c`, obtained by conditioning the reassociated law `((A,B),C)`, is the same as `condFstSndGivenThird`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condFstGivenSnd_pairThirdLaw_eq_condFstSndGivenThird

declarations.doc: Conditional mutual information of the fiber over `C=c`. The value is zero when `P_C(c)=0`; otherwise it is the mutual information of the conditional joint law `P_{A,B|C=c}`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condMutualInfoFstSndGivenThird

declarations.doc: Zero-marginal conditioning atoms contribute zero fiber mutual information.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condMutualInfoFstSndGivenThird_of_thirdMarginal_eq_zero

declarations.doc: On nonzero third-coordinate atoms, the fiber CMI is the mutual information of `P_{A,B|C=c}`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condMutualInfoFstSndGivenThird_of_thirdMarginal_ne_zero

declarations.doc: Conditional mutual information can be rewritten as `H(A|C) + H(B|C) - H(A,B|C)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condMutualInfo_eq_condEntropy_marginals

declarations.doc: On a nonzero third-coordinate atom, fiber CMI is the entropy identity for the three conditional fibers: `I(A;B | C=c) = H(A|C=c) + H(B|C=c) - H(A,B|C=c)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condMutualInfoFstSndGivenThird_eq_entropy_fibers_of_ne_zero

declarations.doc: Weighted pointwise bridge between fiber CMI and the three conditional entropy fibers. The zero-marginal case is killed by the factor `P_C(c)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.thirdMarginal_toReal_mul_condMutualInfoFstSndGivenThird

declarations.doc: Expected conditional mutual information formula: `I(A;B | C) = sum_c P_C(c) I(A;B | C=c)`. The fiber term is zero on zero-marginal `c` and otherwise the mutual information of `P_{A,B|C=c}`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condMutualInfo_eq_sum_thirdMarginal_mul_condMutualInfoFstSndGivenThird

declarations.doc: Real-valued first-marginal mass formula. Mathematically, for a finite joint PMF `p` on `alpha × beta`, `p_A(a) = sum_b p(a,b)`, now stated after converting all finite masses to real numbers.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.fstMarginal_toReal_apply

declarations.doc: Real-valued second-marginal mass formula. Mathematically, for a finite joint PMF `p` on `alpha × beta`, `p_B(b) = sum_a p(a,b)`, now stated after converting all finite masses to real numbers.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.sndMarginal_toReal_apply

declarations.doc: Rewrite an expectation of a first-coordinate observable under the joint law as an expectation under the first marginal: `sum_{a,b} p(a,b) f(a) = sum_a p_A(a) f(a)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.sum_toReal_mul_fstMarginal

declarations.doc: Rewrite an expectation of a second-coordinate observable under the joint law as an expectation under the second marginal: `sum_{a,b} p(a,b) f(b) = sum_b p_B(b) f(b)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.sum_toReal_mul_sndMarginal

declarations.doc: Entropy as the elementary finite sum `sum_a p(a) * (-log p(a))`. This is definitionally the same as the `Real.negMulLog` formula, but this shape is better for comparing entropy identities with KL-divergence sums.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropy_eq_sum_toReal_mul_neg_log

declarations.doc: First-marginal entropy as a joint-law expectation: `H(A) = sum_{a,b} p(a,b) * (-log p_A(a))`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropy_fstMarginal_eq_sum_joint

declarations.doc: Second-marginal entropy as a joint-law expectation: `H(B) = sum_{a,b} p(a,b) * (-log p_B(b))`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropy_sndMarginal_eq_sum_joint

declarations.doc: Mutual information written as the joint expectation of the three entropy summands: `I(A;B) = sum_{a,b} p(a,b) * (-log p_A(a) + -log p_B(b) - -log p(a,b))`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfo_eq_sum_neg_log

declarations.doc: Pointwise algebra behind the KL formula for mutual information. For every atom `(a,b)`, the weighted entropy summand equals `p(a,b) * log (p(a,b) / (p_A(a) * p_B(b)))`. If `p(a,b)=0`, both sides are zero; otherwise nonzero joint mass forces both marginal masses to be nonzero.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfo_summand_eq_log_ratio

declarations.doc: Finite log-ratio formula for mutual information: `I(A;B) = sum_{a,b} p(a,b) log (p(a,b) / (p_A(a) p_B(b)))`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfo_eq_sum_log_ratio

declarations.doc: Countable PMF density formula. If `p.toMeasure ≪ q.toMeasure`, then `p.toMeasure` is obtained from `q.toMeasure` by the density `a ↦ p(a) / q(a)`. On atoms with `q(a)=0`, absolute continuity forces `p(a)=0`, so the formula is still valid.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.toMeasure_withDensity_pmf_div

declarations.doc: Finite KL expansion for absolutely continuous PMFs: `D(P || Q) = sum_a p(a) log (p(a) / q(a))`. The left-hand side is mathlib's measure-theoretic KL divergence applied to `PMF.toMeasure`; the right-hand side is the usual finite sum over atoms.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.toReal_klDiv_pmf_eq_sum

declarations.doc: Semantic bridge for finite mutual information: `I(A;B) = D(P_AB || P_A × P_B)`. Here `P_A × P_B` is represented by the finite independent-product PMF `indepProd (fstMarginal p) (sndMarginal p)`. This theorem is the finite textbook identity connecting the entropy definition of mutual information to mathlib's measure-theoretic KL divergence.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfo_eq_toReal_klDiv_joint_indepProd

declarations.doc: Product-measure form of the finite mutual-information KL bridge: `I(A;B) = D(P_AB || P_A.toMeasure.prod P_B.toMeasure)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfo_eq_toReal_klDiv_joint_prod_marginals

declarations.doc: Fiberwise KL form of conditional mutual information. This is zero on zero-marginal third-coordinate atoms. On nonzero atoms it is `D(P_{A,B|C=c} || P_{A|C=c} × P_{B|C=c})`, using the marginals of the conditional joint law.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condMutualInfoFstSndGivenThirdKL

declarations.doc: The fiber mutual-information helper agrees with the fiberwise KL expression `D(P_{A,B|C=c} || P_{A|C=c} × P_{B|C=c})`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condMutualInfoFstSndGivenThird_eq_KL

declarations.doc: Averaged conditional-KL bridge for finite conditional mutual information: `I(A;B | C) = sum_c P_C(c) D(P_{A,B|C=c} || P_{A|C=c} × P_{B|C=c})`. Zero-marginal `c` contribute zero by definition of `condMutualInfoFstSndGivenThirdKL`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condMutualInfo_eq_sum_thirdMarginal_mul_condMutualInfoFstSndGivenThirdKL

declarations.doc: Independent product of two probability mass functions. Mathematically, this is the law of `(X, Y)` when `X` has law `p`, `Y` has law `q`, and the two variables are independent.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.indepProd

declarations.doc: The atom mass of an independent product is the product of the two atom masses.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.indepProd_apply

declarations.doc: An atom of the independent product has zero mass exactly when one of the two coordinate atoms has zero mass.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.indepProd_apply_eq_zero_iff

declarations.doc: An atom belongs to the independent product support exactly when both coordinate atoms belong to the corresponding supports.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.indepProd_apply_ne_zero_iff

declarations.doc: The support of an independent product is the product of the two supports.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.support_indepProd

declarations.doc: The first marginal of an independent product is the first factor law.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.fstMarginal_indepProd

declarations.doc: The second marginal of an independent product is the second factor law.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.sndMarginal_indepProd

declarations.doc: Swapping the coordinates of an independent product swaps the two factor laws.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.indepProd_map_swap

declarations.doc: The independent product law assigns product rectangles the product of the factor probabilities.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.indepProd_toMeasure_prod

declarations.doc: The measure associated to the independent product PMF is the product of the two associated measures.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.indepProd_toMeasure

declarations.doc: A finite joint PMF is absolutely continuous with respect to the independent product of its two marginals. Mathematically, if the product of the marginal masses of `(a, b)` is zero, then one marginal atom is zero, hence the joint atom `p (a, b)` is zero. This is the support fact needed before comparing finite mutual information with KL divergence from the joint law to the product of its marginals.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.joint_toMeasure_absolutelyContinuous_indepProd_marginals

declarations.doc: Product-measure version of `joint_toMeasure_absolutelyContinuous_indepProd_marginals`. This is the form expected by KL-divergence statements that use `Measure.prod` directly.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.joint_toMeasure_absolutelyContinuous_prod_marginals

declarations.doc: Mutual information is nonnegative: `0 <= I(A;B)`. The proof passes through the semantic bridge `I(A;B) = D(P_AB || P_A x P_B)` and then uses nonnegativity of the real coercion of an `ENNReal` KL divergence.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfo_nonneg

declarations.doc: Fiber conditional entropy is nonnegative: `0 <= H(A | B=b)`. On zero-mass fibers this is `0 <= 0`; on nonzero fibers it reduces to entropy nonnegativity for the conditional law `P_{A|B=b}`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropyFstGivenSnd_nonneg

declarations.doc: Conditional entropy is nonnegative: `0 <= H(A | B)`. The proof uses the finite semantic bridge `H(A | B) = sum_b P_B(b) * H(P_{A|B=b})`, then every summand is nonnegative.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropy_nonneg

declarations.doc: Conditional entropy of finite-valued random variables is nonnegative.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropyOf_nonneg

declarations.doc: Fiber conditional mutual information is nonnegative: `0 <= I(A;B | C=c)`. On zero-mass fibers this is `0 <= 0`; on nonzero fibers it reduces to `mutualInfo_nonneg` applied to the conditional joint law `P_{A,B|C=c}`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condMutualInfoFstSndGivenThird_nonneg

declarations.doc: Conditional mutual information is nonnegative: `0 <= I(A;B | C)`. We use the finite semantic bridge `I(A;B | C) = sum_c P_C(c) * I(A;B | C=c)`, then each summand is nonnegative because both `P_C(c)` and the fiber mutual information are nonnegative.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condMutualInfo_nonneg

declarations.doc: Taking the first marginal after projecting `(A,B,C)` to `(A,C)` recovers the first marginal of the original law: `(P_AC)_A = P_A`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.fstMarginal_fstThirdMarginal

declarations.doc: Viewing a triple law as a joint law of `A` and `(B,C)`, the second marginal is the same law as the explicit `(B,C)` marginal: `P_{B,C} = sndMarginal P_{A,(B,C)}`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.sndMarginal_eq_sndThirdMarginal

declarations.doc: First mutual-information chain rule: `I(A;B,C) = I(A;C) + I(A;B | C)`. In Lean's right-associated product convention, a PMF `p : PMF (alpha × beta × gamma)` is also a joint law of `A` and `(B,C)`, so `mutualInfo p` denotes `I(A;B,C)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfo_chain_rule_fst

declarations.doc: Second mutual-information chain-rule variant: `I(A;B,C) = I(A;B) + I(A;C | B)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfo_chain_rule_snd

declarations.doc: Random-variable form of `mutualInfo_chain_rule_fst`: `I(X;Y,Z) = I(X;Z) + I(X;Y | Z)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfoOf_chain_rule_fst

declarations.doc: Random-variable form of `mutualInfo_chain_rule_snd`: `I(X;Y,Z) = I(X;Y) + I(X;Z | Y)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfoOf_chain_rule_snd

declarations.doc: Conditional mutual information as a difference of conditional entropies: `I(A;B | C) = H(A | C) - H(A | B,C)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condMutualInfo_eq_condEntropy_fstThirdMarginal_sub_condEntropy

declarations.doc: Conditioning reduces entropy: `H(A | B,C) <= H(A | C)`. The proof rewrites the difference as `I(A;B | C)` and uses conditional mutual information nonnegativity.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropy_le_condEntropy_fstThirdMarginal

declarations.doc: Random-variable conditioning-reduces-entropy theorem: `H(X | Y,Z) <= H(X | Z)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropyOf_pair_le_condEntropyOf
